title: Banoti waterfall
tagline: Banoti waterfall — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Banoti waterfall is a remote and desolate location off the village of Banoti in the Soegaon taluka of the Aurangabad district in the Maharashtra state of India.
overview.description: Banoti waterfall is a remote and desolate location off the village of Banoti in the Soegaon taluka of the Aurangabad district in the Maharashtra state of India. This waterfall is least known in spite of being quite picturesque and enchanting. Access to the place is difficult without involving a long trek from the nearest village of Banoti. The importance of the waterfall is linked to archaeology and Buddhism, since there is an ancient Buddhist monastery of the fifth century CE. The monastery (or its remains) is not accessible (except with the help of a climbing rope).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Banoti waterfall?
faq.a: The best time to visit Banoti waterfall is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Banoti waterfall?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Aurangabad Airport (65 km) and the nearest railway station is Aurangabad Railway Station (63 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Banoti waterfall?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Banoti waterfall at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Banoti waterfall?
faq.a: Stays in Banoti waterfall start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Banoti waterfall?
faq.a: The top attractions are Purnaneopur Dam.
